input: Sentence: The Data Link Layer acts as the communication interface between the physical layer and network layer Entity1: physical layer (TOPIC). Entity2: data link layer (TOPIC).

Sentence: Today’s network designs are based on three topologies: • Bus consists of series of computers connected along a single cable segment • Star connects computers via central connection point or hub • Ring connects computers to form a loop All computers, regardless of topology, communicate by addressing data to one or more computers and transmitting it across cable as electronic signals Entity1: Segment (CONCEPT). Entity2: Hub (OTHER).
Sentence: Transport Layer: Handles host-to-host communication (TCP and UDP) Entity1: Transport Layer (TOPIC). Entity2: TCP (OTHER).
Sentence: HTTP is an application layer protocol because it runs on top of TCP and is closely associated with the Web Entity1: protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: http (OTHER).

Sentence: Flow control mechanisms like stop-and-wait or sliding window protocols regulate the rate of transmission between communicating nodes Entity1: flow control (CONCEPT). Entity2: sliding window (METHOD).
Sentence: MAC addressing uniquely identifies each device within a network segment, enabling accurate frame delivery Entity1: frame (CONCEPT). Entity2: segment (CONCEPT).
Sentence: IPv6 addresses the limitations of IPv4 by providing a larger address space and simplified packet processing Entity1: ipv4 (OTHER). Entity2: ipv6 (OTHER).

Sentence: Routing protocols such as Routing Information Protocol, Open Shortest Path First, and Border Gateway Protocol ensure routers maintain accurate routing tables Entity1: protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: gateway (OTHER).

Sentence: However, partially due to the protocol's adoption of QUIC, HTTP/3 has lower latency and loads more quickly in real-world usage when compared with previous versions: in some cases over four times faster than with HTTP/1 Entity1: protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: latency (CONCEPT).
Sentence: The transport layer in computer networks is responsible for providing reliable communication between end systems Entity1: computer networks (TOPIC). Entity2: transport layer (TOPIC).
Sentence: Once packet loss is detected, the protocol reduces its transmission rate to stabilize the network Entity1: protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: packet (CONCEPT).
Sentence: In contrast, User Datagram Protocol (UDP) is connectionless and does not guarantee delivery, order, or error correction Entity1: Protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: Datagram (CONCEPT).

Sentence: This allows tasks and resources to be distributed across different machines, improving performance, reliability, and scalability Entity1: Scalability (CONCEPT). Entity2: Reliability (CONCEPT).
Sentence: Computer networks are operated by network models; most prominently the OSIRM and the TCP/ IP Models Entity1: Network Models (TOPIC). Entity2: TCP (OTHER).
Sentence: IPv6 was developed to overcome IPv4 address exhaustion and provide better scalability for the rapidly growing internet Entity1: ipv4 (OTHER). Entity2: ipv6 (OTHER).

Sentence: Data Link Layer • Is responsible for moving frames from node to node or computer to computer • Can move frames from one adjacent computer to another, cannot move frames across routers • Encapsulation = frame • Requires MAC address or physical address • Protocols defined include Ethernet Protocol and Point-to-Point Protocol (PPP) • Device example: Switch • Two sublayers: Logical Link Control (LLC) and the Media Access Control (MAC) o Logical Link Control (LLC) – Data Link layer addressing, flow control, address notification, error control o Media Access Control (MAC) –Determines which computer has access to the network media at any given time –Determines where one frame ends and the next one starts, called frame synchronization Entity1: Data Link Layer (TOPIC). Entity2: Ethernet (OTHER).
Sentence: DNS acts like a phonebook of the Internet, matching domain names with their corresponding IP addresses Entity1: ip (OTHER). Entity2: dns (OTHER).
Sentence: It also introduces protocols and standards, the OSI model, and the TCP/IP protocol suite Entity1: Protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: IP (OTHER).

Sentence: As a network layer device, the router collects and stores information such as the IP address of its connected devices in a routing table and uses it for packet forwarding to the right destination Entity1: network layer (TOPIC). Entity2: packet (CONCEPT).

Sentence: DNS queries and responses relied on UDP as its transport protocol, based on the rationale that DNS queries and responses needed to be fast and lightweight, and could not handle the corresponding overhead of a TCP three- way handshake Entity1: protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: tcp (OTHER).
Sentence: Each host receives an IP address through Internet Protocol version 4 or Internet Protocol version 6 Entity1: protocol (CONCEPT). Entity2: ip address (CONCEPT).

Sentence: When a router receives a packet, it searches its routing table to find the best match between the destination IP address of the packet and one of the addresses in the routing table Entity1: router (OTHER). Entity2: ip (OTHER).

Sentence: SMTP typically operates over TCP port 25 for server-to-server communication and port 587 or 465 for client- to-server email submission with encryption Entity1: encryption (METHOD). Entity2: smtp (OTHER).
Sentence: HTTPS is HTTP over TLS or SSL, providing encrypted communication and secure identification of a network web server Entity1: https (OTHER). Entity2: ssl (OTHER).
Sentence: Routers filters and forward data based on logical addresses, for example, the IP address Entity1: ip address (CONCEPT). Entity2: ip (OTHER).
